title: 10 best herbal teas for runny nose
intro_desc: Herbal teas for runny nose are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help alleviate symptoms of a runny nose. They can provide relief by reducing inflammation, soothing irritation, and acting as a natural decongestant. Some popular herbal teas for a runny nose include chamomile, ginger, peppermint, and eucalyptus. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. Ginger tea, with its natural decongestant properties, can help clear blocked nasal passages. Peppermint and eucalyptus teas contain menthol, which can help thin mucus and soothe irritated nasal membranes. By incorporating these herbal teas into a daily routine, individuals can find relief from a runny nose, allowing them to breathe easier, sleep better, and overall, improve their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with runny nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The active compounds in yarrow, such as chamazulene and saponins, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and a decrease in mucus production. This natural remedy can provide relief from runny nose symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow tea has antimicrobial compounds that help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate a runny nose caused by allergies or colds.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in yarrow tea help to dry up excess mucus and reduce congestion, providing quick relief from a runny nose and sinus pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate symptoms of a runny nose caused by allergies or environmental irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of yarrow plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain flavonoids, which act as natural antihistamines, helping to relieve nasal congestion and stop excessive mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow leaves and flowers or use dried ones, measuring out one teaspoon for every eight ounces of water needed.
Combine the measured yarrow with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup, allowing it to steep for five to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Add honey or lemon to taste, stirring well to combine before serving, if desired.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with runny nose because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the nasal passages. The herb's mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates, form a protective barrier on the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by colds, allergies, and sinusitis. This allows the body to fight off infection more effectively and reduces excessive mucus production, leading to a clearer and less congested nose.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow tea's main active ingredient, mucilage, forms a protective film on the respiratory tract, helping to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages, reducing congestion and runniness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, which can alleviate symptoms such as congestion, runny nose, and sinus pressure.

Antibacterial properties: Marshmallow tea's antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to a runny nose, promoting a healthy balance of flora in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their mucilage content, which helps to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
Bark: The bark contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal cavity.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the root in boiling water for 10-15 minutes or until desired strength is reached.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or cup.
Discard the solids and add honey to taste if desired, stirring well to combine.
Serve hot, allowing the tea to cool slightly before consumption for optimal flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort teas helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The herbs' active compounds, such as thymol and 8-epi-lambda-cadinene, help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and a decrease in mucus production. Additionally, mugwort's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure associated with runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Mugwort tea contains antihistamines, which help to reduce histamine levels in the body, thereby decreasing nasal congestion and runny nose symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in mugwort tea help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating runny nose and sinus pressure.
Expectorant properties: Mugwort's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of mugwort are used to make medicinal teas because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion and ease sinus pressure.
Flowers: Mugwort flowers are used to make teas due to their antihistamine effects, which help alleviate symptoms of allergies such as a runny nose and itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried mugwort leaves and flowers per eight ounces water.
Bring the water to boiling point in a medium-sized pot and reduce heat to simmer.
Steep the measured mugwort in the hot water for 5-7 minutes or until desired strength achieved.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot.
Serve the hot herbal tea immediately, garnished with lemon slices or honey to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy teas helps with runny nose because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. The soothing qualities of the tea also calm the mucous membranes, reducing excess mucus production and alleviating congestion. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the tea may help to combat environmental stressors that can exacerbate a runny nose, providing relief from constant sniffles and sneezing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in daisies, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can help alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.

Antihistamine-like properties: The chamazulene content in some daisy varieties has been found to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions and congestion in the nose.

Decongestant properties: The volatile oils present in daisies, such as camphor and borneol, can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and reduced congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Seeds: Contain natural expectorants that help loosen mucus and promote drainage, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried bellis perennis flowers and leaves, measuring 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the daisy mixture with boiling water in a teapot or infuser, steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well to dissolve, sweetening the tea slightly.
Allow the tea to cool before serving, enjoying the calming effects of this herbal brew.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The tea's flavonoids and antioxidants also have a decongestant effect, helping to ease excess mucus production and alleviate congestion. Additionally, the tea's soothing qualities can calm irritated tissues and mucous membranes, providing relief from persistent coughing and sneezing associated with runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine effects: Pot marigold's flavonoids, particularly apigenin, exhibit antihistamine-like properties, which can help reduce the body's allergic response and subsequent nasal discharge.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The plant's terpenoids and phenolic acids have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, leading to a decrease in runny nose symptoms.

Antimicrobial effects: Pot marigold's essential oils and flavonoids exhibit antimicrobial activity against various microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can help combat underlying infections or allergies that may be contributing to a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties help reduce nasal congestion and soothe a runny nose.
Seeds: Natural expectorant properties loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and providing relief from a congested nose.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried calendula officinalis flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water needed.
Bring the measured water to a rolling boil in a teapot or kettle.
Add the dried calendula officinalis flowers to the boiling water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flower petals.
Serve the pot marigold tea hot, or let it cool to drink warm or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with runny nose because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, the active compounds in chamomile work to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion. This can help to dry up excess mucus and reduce nasal discharge, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and chamazulene, can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure.

Antihistamine-like effects: The flavonoids present in German Chamomile tea have antihistamine-like properties, which can help counteract the effects of histamines released during an allergic reaction, leading to a reduction in runny nose symptoms.
Soften and loosen mucus: The mucilage contained in German Chamomile tea can help soften and loosen excess mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and chamazulene, which help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Flowers: Contain bisabolol, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure, promoting a clear runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried german chamomile flowers per cup of boiling water.
Pour the measured german chamomile over boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Steep for 5-7 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor profile of tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving hot or chilling in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower teas helps with runny nose because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and excess mucus production. The tea's antimicrobial properties also work to combat underlying infections that can cause a runny nose, promoting healing and relief from sinus pressure and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Antioxidants in Eastern Purple Coneflower tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and runniness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which may help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal tissues, leading to a less runny nose.

Expectorant effects: Eastern Purple Coneflower tea contains expectorants like mucilage, which can help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from excess mucus and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Seeds: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in quantities of one tablespoon per cup of water.
Combine the harvested herbs with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser for steeping.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the desired flavor and medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Enjoy the tea hot or cold within 24 hours of preparation, sweetened with honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties, which work to reduce excess mucus production and ease congestion. The herbal tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and cineole, help to open up airways and clear out debris, providing relief from a stuffy or runny nose. Additionally, the soothing effects of the tea can calm irritated nasal passages, promoting a healthy balance in the respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, leading to reduced runniness.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in the tea, such as quercetin and gallic acid, can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to a runny nose.

Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing the sensation of a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce nasal congestion and relieve sinus pressure.
Bark: The bark has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including runny nose, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh or dried tasmanian blue gum leaves and stalks for every liter of water needed.
Rinse the harvested leaves and stalks with clean filtered water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine the rinsed tasmanian blue gum leaves and stalks with boiling water in a large pot 10-15 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes before straining it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container quickly.
Store the resulting tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days, serving hot or cold as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue Felicia teas helps with runny nose because it contains a unique blend of herbs that work together to combat congestion. The tea's main ingredient, chamomile, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier for your body to clear out excess mucus. Meanwhile, the presence of peppermint and eucalyptus helps loosen and break down phlegm, allowing you to breathe more easily and expel any unwanted moisture from your nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Blue Vervain's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
Expectorant: The tea's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing nasal discharge.
Antihistamine: Blue Vervain contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit antihistaminic effects, which can help alleviate allergic reactions that often cause a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Blue Felicia plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce nasal congestion and relieve symptoms of a runny nose.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the Blue Felicia plant possess antibacterial properties that help combat infections causing nasal discharge, making it an effective treatment for runny noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried blue felicia flowers according to botanical standards weighing 2-3 ounces per batch.
Clean the harvested flowers by gently sieving them through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any debris or stems.
Measure out 8-10 grams of clean blue felicia flowers and add them to a heat-resistant glass teapot or infuser.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the blue felicia flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes before serving hot.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container and discard solids while retaining liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with runny nose because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The tea's expectorant qualities also loosen and clear out excess mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a more manageable flow of nasal secretions. Additionally, licorice root contains compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing quick relief from congestion and sinus pressure. As a result, herbal licorice teas can provide natural and effective relief for runny noses caused by allergies, colds, or other respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing the amount of mucus produced.
Licoric Acid: Has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to combat infections that cause sinusitis and other respiratory issues associated with a runny nose.
Flavonoids: Act as natural decongestants, helping to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can alleviate symptoms such as stuffiness and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to dry up excess mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Bring 8-10 ounces of filtered water to a boil in a kettle or pot.
Steep the licorice root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its flavor and medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Enjoy the warm, sweet-tasting herbal tea immediately, as it can be re-steeped once for added potency.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat runny nose with herbal teas is by choosing teas with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, such as chamomile, ginger, or peppermint. These teas can help alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose. To maximize benefits, drink the tea hot, as the steam can help clear nasal congestion. Additionally, consider adding a squeeze of lemon or a dash of honey for added vitamin C and soothing effects. However, if symptoms persist for more than a week, it is recommended to consult a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal teas include sinus congestion, sore throat, and cough. Herbal teas made from ingredients like chamomile, ginger, peppermint, and echinacea can help soothe irritated nasal passages and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, while ginger and peppermint have natural decongestant and expectorant properties that can help break up mucus and clear nasal congestion. Echinacea, on the other hand, can help boost the immune system and reduce the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms.
